# Pherix

> Pherix is a transactional resource runtime for AI agents — a Python + TypeScript
> library that gives an agent's tool calls database-style ACID guarantees over their
> external side-effects (DB writes, file writes, API calls). Reversible effects run live
> and roll back via real backend semantics (DB savepoints, filesystem copy-on-write);
> irreversible effects are staged, bounded by policy, and either auto-compensated or gated.
> Every capability — commit, rollback, replay, dry-run, isolation, audit — is one
> traversal of a single append-only effect journal.

Pherix is a library you wrap your agent's tool-call layer with. It is **not** an agent
framework, a permission system, or an observability tool: it enforces and contains; it does
not run the agent or call the LLM. Phase 1 (the engine) is built — both lanes, MVCC
isolation, replay, policy, dry-run, the MCP gateway, crash-consistent recovery — with 936
Python tests passing (959 collected) + 214 TypeScript, fully offline.

This file is the curated index. For the complete, executable integration guide in one
fetch, see [llms-full.txt](llms-full.txt).

## Start here

- [llms-full.txt](llms-full.txt): the complete, executable integration recipe — follow it top to bottom to wrap an existing agent in Pherix, with the three silent-failure gotchas spelled out.
- [README](README.md): install, minimal wrap, quickstart with expected output.
- [Project spec (CLAUDE.md)](CLAUDE.md): the full system definition — the journal, the four axes, the execution model, the conventions.

## Concepts & architecture

- [How it works, end to end](site/docs.html): the stack, the journal and its folds, the reversible/irreversible lanes, the two undo mechanisms (adapter restore vs compensator). Served by `python -m http.server` from the repo root.
- [Project spec (CLAUDE.md)](CLAUDE.md): the canonical system definition — the journal, the four axes (interception · policy · adapters · compensators), the execution model.
- [Replay recipe](docs/replay-recipe.md): folding the journal forward against fresh state to re-derive or verify a run.

## Examples

- examples/quickstart.py: the smallest correct integration, verified end-to-end in a fresh venv.
- examples/demos/undo.py: the flagship — a wrong-but-allowed bulk write rolled back live via `ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT` (40k rows restored, 0 lost).
- examples/demos/{gate,isolation,authority,lineage}.py: one runnable demo per engine capability — gated irreversible + approve-over-the-wire, MVCC conflict rejection, authority tiers over the same purge, read→write provenance from the journal.

## Optional

- TypeScript SDK (pherix-ts/): 14 adapters at semantic + field-name parity with Python; a parity suite proves both engines produce structurally identical journals.
